data.title: Tom Lee warns of digital asset bubble burst, says valuations collapsing. DAT trend losing steam, firms trading below asset value.
data.description: Tom Lee, the chairman of BitMine Immersion Technologies Inc. (BMNR), has raised concerns regarding the digital asset treasuries trend, noting a significant decline in valuations. In a recent interview on Fortune's Crypto Playbook podcast, Lee highlighted that the digital asset treasury (DAT) sector, which saw a surge over the past two years, is losing momentum. These companies, which function as public entities holding large reserves of cryptocurrencies, have been likened to Strategy Inc. (MSTR), known for accumulating Bitcoin (BTC) for corporate reserves starting in 2020. Lee pointed out that many of these firms are currently trading below their inherent asset value, indicating a shift in investor sentiment towards DATs. The full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Worksport (WKSP) reports record Q3 revenue of $5.02 million, up 62% YoY, gross profit up 548%. Full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Worksport (ticker symbol: WKSP) released its Q3 2025 unaudited financial results, unveiling a record-breaking quarterly revenue and gross profit. The clean energy firm reported $5.02 million in quarterly revenue, a 62% increase year-over-year and 22% higher than the previous quarter. Though slightly below the projected $5.58 million, the company's impressive performance exceeded expectations. Additionally, Worksport's gross profit soared by 548% compared to the previous year. Further insights can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: The Connell Company starts building second tower at The Park in Berkeley Heights, NJ, transforming it into a premier destination.
data.description: Berkeley Heights, New Jersey, October 16, 2025 - The Connell Company, a major privately-held corporation in the U.S., has initiated the construction of RT600, a development pivotal in The Park's evolution into a dynamic hub for living, working, entertainment, and accommodation. This project signifies a crucial milestone in the transformation of the area.

data.title: Abbott's Q3 sales slightly miss consensus at $11.37B, narrowing 2025 guidance to $5.12-$5.18 per share. Analysts affirm medtech strength amidst noise, highlighting 10% EPS growth heading into 2026.
data.description: Abbott Laboratories (NYSE: ABT) announced on Wednesday that its third-quarter sales totaled $11.37 billion, slightly lower than the expected $11.40 billion. The company also revised its fiscal 2025 adjusted earnings guidance, now projected to be in the range of $5.12 to $5.18 per share, down from the previous estimate of $5.10 to $5.20 per share. Analysts noted that the financial statement was somewhat turbulent, leading to a drop in share prices. William Blair's Brandon Vazquez expressed confidence in Abbott's position in the medical technology sector, emphasizing the 10% EPS growth forecasted for 2026. Prominent growth drivers for the company moving forward include the significant impact of diagnostics, anticipated to exceed $1 billion. For more details, the full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: White Castle unveils new prototype design enhancing customer experience, comfort, efficiency in Columbus; celebrates signature style.
data.description: A recent unveiling of a prototype restaurant design by White Castle in Columbus, Ohio on October 16, 2025, aims to improve customer experience, enhance team member comfort, and boost operational efficiency. White Castle, known as America's original fast-food hamburger chain, showcases its signature style through this innovative redesign. The new design combines comfort and efficiency, offering a unique experience for patrons and showcasing White Castle's renowned charm.

data.title: Vantage Drilling Intl. Ltd. holds successful annual general meeting in Dubai, all proposed matters approved.
data.description: Today in Dubai, Vantage Drilling International Ltd. held its annual general meeting at the law offices of Conyers Dill & Pearman Limited in Bermuda. All proposed matters were approved at the meeting.

data.title: NYU Langone Health study: Smartphone app for muscle relaxation reduces disability for ED migraine patients.
data.description: On October 16, 2025, researchers from NYU Langone Health conducted a study showing that a smartphone app designed for muscle relaxation led to a notable decrease in migraine-related disability among patients in the emergency department. The study highlighted that the app played a significant role in reducing disability levels in patients suffering from migraines. The research findings suggest promising outcomes for utilizing technology to improve patient care, with the app showcasing effectiveness in enhancing relaxation techniques for individuals experiencing migraines. This study underscores the potential impact of smartphone apps in managing and alleviating migraine symptoms, pointing towards a potentially valuable tool in healthcare settings.

data.title: Defamation settlement affirms integrity of Dr. James MacDonald, Founder of Act Like Men Ministries, reinforcing truth in public commentary.
data.description: In a recent legal development in Chicago on October 16, 2025, it was stated that Dr. James MacDonald, the founding Pastor of Harvest Bible Chapel (HBC), has successfully upheld the importance of truth in public commentary. This legal resolution not only affirms MacDonald's integrity but also strengthens the mission of HBC going forward. The details of the legal proceedings remain undisclosed, but the outcome underscores the commitment to upholding honesty and integrity in public discourse. This marks a significant victory for Dr. MacDonald and his legal team, consolidating their stance on maintaining truthfulness in all public communications.

data.title: Canadian General Investments, Ltd. declares $0.27 per share dividend for common shareholders, payable on Dec. 15, 2025.
data.description: In Toronto, Canada on October 16, 2025, Canadian General Investments, Limited (CGI) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.27 per share. The dividend will be paid on December 15, 2025, to common shareholders who are recorded as such by the close of business on November 28, 2025. This dividend is classified as a capital gains dividend under the Income Tax Act (Canada).

data.title: Amazon stock slightly lower, announces telehealth service expansion for parents, covers kid conditions with pay-per-visit model. No insurance needed.
data.description: Shares of Amazon.com Inc (NASDAQ: AMZN) experienced a slight dip in trading on Thursday as the company revealed its plan to expand its telehealth service. The expansion of the Amazon One Medical Pay-per-visit service will now cater to parents and guardians seeking care for common childhood conditions through a pay-per-visit model. This service will connect families with board-certified family care providers and will now encompass a wider range of conditions including pink eye, head lice, eczema, contact dermatitis, ringworm, bug bites, and prescription renewals for EpiPen and asthma medications. Notably, this service does not require insurance, Prime membership, or One Medical membership for access. Each visit will provide a personalized treatment plan along with follow-up support for 14 days, enabling patients to seek clarifications on treatment. This announcement comes ahead of the company's earnings later this month. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson's Phase 3 trial shows Tecvayli-Darzalex combo benefits multiple myeloma patients after 3 prior treatments.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) recently disclosed positive top-line outcomes from the Phase 3 MajesTEC-3 trial. This study assessed the effectiveness of Tecvayli (teclistamab-cqyv) in conjunction with Darzalex Faspro (daratumumab and hyaluronidase-fihj) in patients dealing with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who had undergone one to three prior treatments. The data revealed that after close to three years of observation, the combination of Tecvayli and Darzalex led to a significant enhancement in both progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) when compared to the existing standard treatments. For more details, refer to the full article accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: PowerPay achieves milestone with one of the Largest Home Improvement ABS Deals on Oct. 16, 2025, as announced.
data.description: In Wayne, Pennsylvania on October 16, 2025, PowerPay, a leading financial technology company focused on point-of-sale consumer financing for home improvement and patient finance, revealed its plans to release its newest Asset-Backed Securitization (ABS) soon. This platform is known for specializing in providing financing solutions for these sectors.

data.title: Eric Trump estimates Trump family made $1B+ profit from crypto, amid conflicts criticism.
data.description: Eric Trump stated that President Trump's family has generated profits exceeding $1 billion from their cryptocurrency ventures amidst mounting criticism regarding potential conflicts of interest.
